content.problemStatement:
Given two words AAA, BBB and a set WWW consisting of MMM distinct words W1,W2,‚Ä¶,WMW_1, W_2, \dots, W_MW1‚Äã,W2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,WM‚Äã. Every word has same length and consists of lowercase english characters.

A **transformation sequence** from word AAA to word BBB using a set of words WWW is a sequence of words A‚ÜíS1‚ÜíS2‚Üí‚ãØ‚ÜíSl‚ÜíBA \rightarrow S_1 \rightarrow S_2 \rightarrow \dots \rightarrow S_l \rightarrow BA‚ÜíS1‚Äã‚ÜíS2‚Äã‚Üí‚ãØ‚ÜíSl‚Äã‚ÜíB such that,


Every adjacent pair of words differs by a single letter.
Every SiS_iSi‚Äã for 1‚â§i‚â§l1 \leq i \leq l1‚â§i‚â§l should be in WWW. Note that AAA, BBB does not need to be in WWW.
Find the number of words in the **shortest transformation sequence** from AAA to BBB, or ‚àí1-1‚àí1 if no such sequence present.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated words AAA, BBB.
Then next line each test case contains integer MMM ‚Äî number of distinct words in WWW.
The next line contain MMM space-separated words WiW_iWi‚Äã ‚Äî the ithi^{th}ith word.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the number of words in the **shortest transformation sequence** from AAA to BBB, or ‚àí1-1‚àí1 if no such sequence present.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: As AAA, BBB both same words so the shortest transformation sequence will have one word chef only.
Test case 222: The shortest transformation sequence will be abc ‚Üí\rightarrow‚Üí abd ‚Üí\rightarrow‚Üí acd ‚Üí\rightarrow‚Üí acb. So there will be 444 words in the sequence.
Test case 333: The shortest transformation sequence will be a ‚Üí\rightarrow‚Üí j.
